title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: June 26, 2026 – Market rebounds from early tech sell-off, AI valuations scrutinized. Not financial advice.

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Mixed as Tech Sector Recovers from Early Jitters
items.summary: U.S. equities experienced a mixed session today, initially weighed down by a global tech sell-off and concerns over artificial intelligence (AI) valuations and a reported delay in OpenAI's IPO. However, major indices showed signs of recovery throughout the day. Easing oil prices provided some support, contributing to a rotation of investor capital into sectors beyond technology. The Dow Jones Industrial Average managed a gain, while the S&P 500 finished nearly flat, and the Nasdaq Composite closed slightly lower, still contending with tech-related pressures.

items.headline: SanDisk Soars on Strong Momentum
items.summary: Memory product maker SanDisk ($SNDK) experienced a significant jump today, rising 22.0%, building on robust returns over the last month.

items.headline: ServiceNow Bucks Tech Trend with Strong Gains
items.summary: Cloud computing company ServiceNow ($NOW) advanced by 7.4% to $96.19, demonstrating strength amidst a broader tech rout.

items.headline: Microsoft Provides Lift to Major Indices
items.summary: Technology giant Microsoft ($MSFT) rose nearly 5% today, contributing significantly to the performance of all three major indices.

items.headline: ON Semiconductor Plunges on Acquisition News
items.summary: ON Semiconductor ($ON) tumbled 21.6% today following its agreement to acquire Synaptics in an all-stock deal valued at approximately $7 billion.

items.headline: SoftBank Falls Amid OpenAI IPO Delay Report
items.summary: SoftBank Group ($SFTBY), a major investor in OpenAI, saw its shares decline by 9.2% amid reports that OpenAI is considering delaying its initial public offering until 2027.

items.headline: Hewlett Packard Enterprises Slides
items.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprises Co ($HPE) fell 6.8% today, continuing a third-straight daily slide, with the catalyst for the drop unclear.

items.headline: OpenAI IPO Delay Reports Fuel Tech Sector Weakness
items.summary: Reports suggest that OpenAI is considering delaying its initial public offering until 2027 as CEO Sam Altman reportedly seeks a $1 trillion valuation. This news significantly impacted the tech sector, contributing to a global sell-off and raising concerns about AI stock valuations and associated costs.

items.headline: Jobless Claims Decrease, Durable Goods Orders Fall
items.summary: Initial jobless claims for the week ending June 20 came in at 215,000, a decrease of 12,000 from the previous week. However, durable goods orders for May fell by 4.5%, suggesting a potential slowdown in manufacturing activity.

items.headline: Inflation Remains Above 4%, Fed Rate Hike Possibility
items.summary: U.S. inflation climbed to 4.1% in May, up from 3.8% in April, marking the first time in three years it has risen above 4%. This sustained inflation keeps alive the possibility of a Federal Reserve rate hike, despite a recent cooling of expectations due to easing oil prices and some economic data.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Shows Slight Improvement Amidst High Cost of Living Concerns
items.summary: The University of Michigan's consumer sentiment survey for June registered 49.5, slightly exceeding estimates. However, the reading remains near historic lows, indicating ongoing concerns among consumers about the high cost of living.

items.headline: Investors Rotate Capital Out of Tech into Broader Market
items.summary: Amid ongoing tech sector weakness and profit-taking in high-flying AI stocks, investors are observed rotating their money into sectors beyond technology. Industrials, energy, and healthcare showed strength today, a development seen as potentially healthy for market breadth.
